title: Shock absorbers in the entry plug
display_title: Shock absorbers in the entry plug
wikitext:
[]: Shock absorbers in the entry plug?             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu Mar 03, 2011 4:35 pm One thing that Mazinger Z set straight, from the very start, was that the actual weak point of a giant robot is its pilot. (And if you bear the idea that such seminal robot genre anime might play like stiff sentai clones, I can definitely suggest you read to read their "origin stories" to see how frequent these EVA-type modern themes were.) The trope was played straight: a single fall, meaning the robot was pushed to the ground with the pilot inside, could send the pilot into shock\coma\death. Now, the entry plug. Pratically every serious hit by an angel has a microscene of the pilot eating the aftershocks. But actual shock absorbers in the piloting unit? For mere walking purposes. I wonder if the synchro ratio does also protect against inertia.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Thu Mar 03, 2011 4:36 pm There's probably some fidgety techno thing in there no doubt.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 03, 2011 5:24 pm Shock absorbers in the entry plug?

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Thu Mar 03, 2011 5:33 pm Reichu wrote: LCL, is probably the extent of the answer you'll get.

[]: Re: Shock absorbers in the entry plug?             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Fri Mar 04, 2011 1:01 am xdiesp wrote: I wonder if the synchro ratio does also protect against inertia.

[]: Re: Shock absorbers in the entry plug?             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 04, 2011 1:14 am nomis1242 wrote: As for shock absorbers etc, there's nothing mentioned in NGE so feel free to invent your own reality!

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Fri Mar 04, 2011 5:16 am ^ haha, I see what you did there... (Sorry to be a party pooper, but the fluid shock absorber only works if the object is suspended in it by buoyancy. Having the pilot sit in the seat means it doesn't help. I think it would be acceptible to fanwank that either the seat is isolated or the entry plug has an outer and inner shell and these are separated by some shock isolation system)

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Mar 04, 2011 6:20 am But if LCL acted as an absorber, Gendo wouldn't freak out about Rei's activation test ejection. That probably shook her around less than most Angel battles.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Fri Mar 04, 2011 7:25 am Just to explain what I meant by synchro ratio as shock absorber. Because yes, it would be logical to guess there's hydraulic amortizers somewhere in the plug around the outer seat. But we've seen synchro ration cases that could explain it: consider the case of Bardiel strangling Shinji. Obviously there's "some" of the clutch strenght being translated to the pilot: but is it supposed to be scaled down? How about the actual 100t pressure applied to Shinji's face? Maybe in a way, the shaking and jumping of the EVA applies almost as the pilot were making professional acrobatics.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Mar 04, 2011 1:19 pm Reichu wrote: LCL, is probably the extent of the answer you'll get.

[]: irony don't rage             Quote Post by xdiesp » Sat Mar 05, 2011 8:53 am

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 05, 2011 10:51 am And on that note, I attempted a split .

(And if you bear the idea that such seminal robot genre anime might play like stiff sentai clones, I can definitely suggest you read to read their "origin stories" to see how frequent these EVA-type modern themes were.) The trope was played straight: a single fall, meaning the robot was pushed to the ground with the pilot inside, could send the pilot into shock\coma\death. Now, the entry plug. Pratically every serious hit by an angel has a microscene of the pilot eating the aftershocks. But actual shock absorbers in the piloting unit? For mere walking purposes. I wonder if the synchro ratio does also protect against inertia.
